expected_facts: ML proof execution must block when required ML problem-selection inputs are missing.
The response should include missing_inputs and a message about objective and dataset profiles.
guidelines: Do not run ML training without the required objective and dataset-profile inputs.

document.content: execute_ml_skill_for_usecase returns execution_blocked when required ML inputs are missing.

expected_facts: PySpark proof execution requires transform_code, code, or artifact_code.
Missing transform code should return execution_blocked.
The message should direct the user to bind generated code before running the proof.
guidelines: Do not synthesize PySpark transform code inside the executor as a fallback.

document.content: execute_pyspark_skill_for_usecase blocks when transform_code/code/artifact_code is not bound.

expected_facts: AI proof status is derived from the skill runtime registry in the current implementation.
The proof result should say it checks contracts and runtime adapters rather than bypassing skills.
runtime_ready means contracts and runtime adapters are available.
guidelines: Do not claim AI execution was performed when the proof only checked the runtime registry.

document.content: _runtime_registry_proof checks BrickVision skill contracts and runtime adapters for AI.
